destinations.evidence: The verifier clone used for spawned verification work shares the same Arc<RwLock<ExecutorRegistry>> instead of copying registry contents.
The registry remains an Arc-backed shared object exposed by reference, matching the shared-state model used by clone_for_task.

equivalence_argument: The current port does not expose a public Clone impl, but every internal verifier clone used by batch verification is created through clone_for_task, which Arc::clone shares the exact registry lock with the source verifier.
notes: Mapped via the current clone_for_task path; no independent registry snapshot is created.
rationale: The manual Clone implementation was updated to Arc::clone the executor_registry field, matching the new constructor-owned registry model and preserving shared validation state across verifier clones. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Cloning an SpcVerifier must preserve access to the same ExecutorRegistry state rather than creating an independent registry snapshot.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create a verifier, clone it, mutate the registry through the original verifier's registry reference to activate an executor, and assert validate_executor on the clone observes the newly active executor.
summary: Internal SPC verifier clones share the same executor registry Arc.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create a verifier, clone it, mutate the registry through the original verifier's registry reference to activate an executor, and assert validate_executor on the clone observes the newly active executor.
title: Cloned SPC verifiers share executor registry state
